topics.topic_title: Ifisumino na Intambi Shacaalo (National Values and Heritage)

sub_topics.title: Ukumfwa na Ukulanda - Ukushimika Ilyashi (Listening and Speaking - Reporting a Story)
competences: Ukubomfya amashiwi ayayaana pakushimika ilyashi illipeelwe pamulandu 'Ukukobekela Namasambililo'
Ukulondolola ifyo ifisumino na intambi shacaalo shingabacingilila mumicitile
Ukubomfya Icibemba icasuminishiwa

instructional_blocks.hook: Ukulanshanya palyashi pakati kakokwa Mulenga nabanankwe
teacher_steps: Biika abasambi mumabumba
Eba abasambi babelenge umulandu uuli pali Mulenga nabanankwe
Balanshanye umulandu uuli pali Mulenga nabanankwe
instructional_blocks.short_notes: Learners discuss the story about Mulenga and his friends regarding the topic 'Ukukobekela Namasambililo' (Peer Pressure and Schooling). The story explores themes of peer pressure among school youth.

instructional_blocks.hook: Ukulondolola ifyalanshanishiwa palyashi pakati ka kwa Mulenga nabanankwe
teacher_steps: Eba abeminishi muli cila ibumba ukulondolola ifyalanshinishiwa palyashi ukulingana na umutwe
learner_steps: Abeminishi muli cilabumba balondolola ifyalanshaninishwa palyashi ukulingana na umutwe
instructional_blocks.short_notes: Group representatives present summaries of what was discussed in the story.

sub_topics.short_notes: This sub-topic focuses on listening and speaking skills through the story 'Ukukobekela Namasambililo' (Peer Pressure and Schooling). Learners explore the theme of peer pressure among youth, national values, and heritage using appropriate vocabulary.

vocabulary.definition: friends (close friends/peers)
close friends, inseparable companions
peer pressure, influence from peers
things related to going home/native activities
longing, desire, yearning
just for nothing, without reason
were making his mouth water

questions: Umutwe walyashi walanshanishiwa bwino (The theme of the story was discussed correctly)

sub_topics.title: Ukubelenga - Amashiwi (Reading - Vocabulary/Words)
competences: Bomfya amashiwi ukulingana na fomu (Use words according to their forms/parts of speech)

instructional_blocks.hook: Ukulanshanya pabupilibulo bwamashiwi yasalilwe mulyashi 'Ukukobekela Namasambililo'
teacher_steps: Biika abasambi mumabumba
Eba abasambi Ukulanshanya pabupilibulo bwamashiwi yasalilwe mulyashi
instructional_blocks.short_notes: Learners discuss the meanings of selected vocabulary words from the listening and speaking story.

teacher_steps: Biika abasambi mumabumba
Eba abasambi balondolole ubupilibulo bwamashiwi kubasambi banabo
instructional_blocks.short_notes: Learners present word meanings to each other.

teacher_steps: Eba cilamusambi abomfye amashiwi yasalilwe mumiseela
instructional_blocks.short_notes: Individual learners use selected vocabulary words in sentences.

sub_topics.short_notes: This sub-topic covers reading comprehension with focus on vocabulary. Learners read and understand passages about national values and heritage, using varied vocabulary in appropriate contexts.

vocabulary.definition: values, principles that guide conduct
heritage, traditions passed down through generations
compass, guide
direction, destination
challenges, problems
disagreement, conflict
agreement, togetherness
platforms used for online communication and sharing
policy aimed at preventing gender-based discrimination
law governing safe and responsible use of the internet

questions: Mumashiwi yobe kuti walondolola shani amashiwi yakuti 'National Value'?
Ukusunga ifipe fyacintu-bwingi ifili nga ifibombelo fya ZESCO, ama desiki yesukulu fiilangililo fyafintu icaalo casuminamo. Bufi/Cishinka.
Mukutontonkanya koobe busumanshi bwaba mufisumino fyacaalo ubukumine ku masambililo?
Kalemba atiila, 'Uwaikete fibili afwile ku Meenshi', londolola ifintu fibili ifyo aleelandapo ifyo abalumendo baleetontonkanyapo.
Lishiwi nshi limbi mumulandu ililepilibula cimo cine na 'ukupanika'?

sub_topics.title: Ukubelenga Mukwikatisha (Fluency Reading)
competences: Belenga imilandu iyalekana lekana ukwabula ukutamantika iyakulinga fomu 1

teacher_steps: Eba cilamusambi abelenge umulandu mukwikatisha ukwabula ukutamantama
learner_steps: Cilamusambi abelenge umulandu mukwikatisha ukwabula ukutamantama
instructional_blocks.short_notes: Learners practice reading texts fluently without hesitation.

teacher_steps: Peela cila musambi icipande cakubelenga mukwikatisha
Eba cilamusambi abelenge umulandu mukwikatisha ukwabula ukutamantama
Ipusha abasambi ameepusho mukulandafye
learner_steps: Cilamusambi abelenge umulandu mukwikatisha ukwabula ukutamantama
instructional_blocks.short_notes: Learners answer oral comprehension questions after silent reading.

sub_topics.short_notes: Good reading habits include: reading fluently without hesitation (mukwikatisha), not reading too fast, reading at an appropriate pace, pronouncing words clearly, showing appropriate facial expressions, and showing intonation at pauses and questions.

questions: Ameepusho yamukulandafye yaasukwa bwino (Oral comprehension questions answered correctly)

sub_topics.title: Ukulemba - Ukuleemba Ilyashi Lyakulondolola Umuntu (Writing - Descriptive Writing of a Person)
competences: Ukuleemba umulandu uulelodolola abantu

instructional_blocks.hook: Ukulanshanya pamilembele na ifipoope fyakukonka pakuleemba ilyashi lyakulondolola umuntu nangu icintu
teacher_steps: Biika abasambi mumabumba
Eba abasambi balanshanye pamilembele nefifwaikwa mukuleemba ilyashi lyakulondolola
learner_steps: Balanshanye pamilembele nefifwaikwa mukuleemba ilyashi lyakulondolola umuntu nangu icintu
instructional_blocks.short_notes: Learners discuss the features and requirements of writing a descriptive composition about a person or thing.

teacher_steps: Eba cilamusambi ukulemba ilyashi lyakulondolola umuntu
instructional_blocks.short_notes: Learners write a descriptive composition about a person based on a given prompt.

sub_topics.short_notes: Descriptive writing of a person should include: Introduction (name, gender, age, origin, duration of acquaintance, occupation, religious affiliation); Physical appearance (height, build, hairstyle, smile); Occupation and education; Character traits (likes, dress style, food, hobbies); and Conclusion (writer's personal views). Use vivid adjectives and verbs to bring the person to life for the reader.

vocabulary.definition: Introduction paragraph of a composition
Character traits, personal attributes
Conclusion of a composition
adjectives used to describe nouns
adverbs used to describe actions

questions: Ilyashi lyakulondolola umuntu lyalembwa bwino (Descriptive composition of a person written correctly)

sub_topics.title: Ukusoobolola Icibemba - Ukubomfya Inundwa (Grammar - Using Prefixes and Suffixes)
competences: Bomfya inundwa shakukutampa na kumpela yeshiwi pakupanga amashiwi

teacher_steps: Londolwela abasambi ubupilibulo na imibomfeshe yanundwa
instructional_blocks.short_notes: Teacher explains prefixes and suffixes, learners identify and discuss their uses.

teacher_steps: Biika abasambi mumabumba
Eba abasambi balanshanye imisango yalekana-lekana iyanundwa
Eba umwiminishi alondolole ifilanshanishiwe
instructional_blocks.short_notes: Learners identify and classify different types of prefixes and suffixes.

teacher_steps: Eba abasambi babiike inundwa shalekana-lekana mumisango yashiko
instructional_blocks.short_notes: Learners place prefixes and suffixes into their correct patterns/categories.

teacher_steps: Eba cilamusambi apange amashiwi ukubomfya inundwa shalekana-lekana
learner_steps: Cilamusambi apange amashiwi ukubomfya inundwa shalekana-lekana
instructional_blocks.short_notes: Individual learners form words using various prefixes and suffixes.

sub_topics.short_notes: Inundwa (Morpheme/Affix): A word part added to a root word that changes or adds meaning. Types: Inundwa shapakutampa kweshiwi (Prefixes) - e.g., aba-kashana, umu-ntu, ici-puna, ifi-puna, ili-ni. Inundwa shakumpela yeshiwi (Suffixes) - e.g., uku-sos-a, uku-nw-a, uku-memeng-a, uku-beleng-a. Note: Proper nouns (names of people and places) and uncountable nouns do not take prefixes.

vocabulary.definition: A word part (morpheme/affix) added to a root word that changes or extends its meaning
Prefixes - word parts added to the beginning of a root word
Suffixes - word parts added to the end of a root word
Nouns - naming words in Icibemba grammar

questions: Biika inundwa mumisango yashiko - Inundwa sha pakutampa kweshiwi elyo ne nundwa sha ku mpela yeshiwi
Panga amashiwi ukubomfya inundwa ishalekana-lekana: Inundwa: umu, aba, a, meenshi, ng'anda, ici, aka, muti, bi, ifi, fi

sub_topics.title: Amashiwi Yakwata Ubupilibulo Bwafisama - Insoselo (Figurative Language - Idioms/Proverbs)
competences: Ukubomfya insoselo (Use proverbs/idioms appropriately)

teacher_steps: Biika abasambi mumabumba
Eba abasambi ukulanshanya pabupilibulo bwansoselo nefyo shibomfiwa
Umwiminishi alondolole ifilanshanishiwe
instructional_blocks.short_notes: Learners discuss the meanings and uses of proverbs such as: Bunamulya pungunyu, Ukufwila ulwisha, Ukufwa icanyongolo, Ukufwika amala yambushi mumukoshi, Katyetye mwenda mwalimwa, Namwelepete isembe lyamukuba.

instructional_blocks.hook: Ukubiika insoselo mumabumba ukulingana neefyo shibomfiwa
teacher_steps: Biika abasambi mumabumba
Eba abasambi babiike insoselo mumabumba ukulingana neefyo shibomfiwa
learner_steps: Babiike insoselo mumabumba ukulingana neefyo shibomfiwa
instructional_blocks.short_notes: Learners classify proverbs into categories: Ukuiletelela (Self-reliance), Ubonaushi (Laziness), Ulufyengo (Deceit), Ubunang'ani (Greediness), Ubusuma bwa kwipusha (Wisdom of asking).

teacher_steps: Eba cila musambi ukusobolola insoselo ukulingana nenshita shingabomba
learner_steps: Cilamusambi asoobolole insosele ukulingana nenshita shingabomba
instructional_blocks.short_notes: Learners match proverbs to appropriate situations/contexts.

teacher_steps: Biika abasambi mumabumba
Eba abasambi bafwaye insoselo shingabofiwa ukulingana nenshita
learner_steps: Bafwaye insoselo ukulingana nenshita shipeelwe peesamba
instructional_blocks.short_notes: Learners find appropriate proverbs for given situations.

sub_topics.short_notes: Insoselo (Proverbs/Idioms) are expressions with figurative meaning that differ from the literal meaning of the words used. Key differences between insoselo and amapinda (idioms): Insoselo consist of a single complete sentence; they have a figurative/deep meaning (ubupilibulo bwayanguka ukumfwa); they are written as one complete sentence. Amapinda have two parts (ukutampa - beginning, and ukupwishishisha - ending); they can have both literal and deep meaning.

vocabulary.definition: Proverb/idiom - a figurative expression that carries deeper meaning beyond its literal words
Idioms with two parts (a setup and completion), usually expressing deeper cultural meaning
Figurative meaning - meaning that is not literal but symbolic or cultural

questions: Leemba ificitiika umo tubomfya ishi nsoselo: i. Kabuusha takolelwe bowa, ii. Uwakanwa takutula, iii. Ukupaapa kalama, iv. Ukuilapwila umulilo pamakasa, v. Amalibu yakuilombela, vi. Ukukulikila imbushi pamimena

sub_topics.title: Ukwalula Ululimi (Translation)
competences: Ukubomfya inshila ishalekana-lekana pakupilibula umulandu ukufuma mucingeleshi ukutwala mucibemba elyo na ukumya mucibemba ukutwala mucingeleshi

instructional_blocks.hook: Ukwalula imiseela ili peesamba ukufuma mu Cingeleshi ukutwala mu Cibemba
teacher_steps: Eba abasambi baalule imiseela ili peesamba ukufuma mu Cingeleshi ukutwala mu Cibemba
learner_steps: Cila musambi aalule imiseela ipeele ukutwala mu Cibemba ukubomfya inshila shimo-shimo isho basambilila
instructional_blocks.short_notes: Learners translate sentences from English to Icibemba using various translation techniques.

sub_topics.short_notes: Translation techniques used in Icibemba: (a) Generalization (ukupilibula ukubomfya ishiwi ilyaishibikwa kubengi) - using a widely known word when there is no direct equivalent. (b) Description (ukulundulula/ukulondolola amashiwi) - describing the concept rather than translating directly. Other techniques include: literal translation, borrowing (ukwashima), and transliteration.

vocabulary.definition: Translation by using a broadly known equivalent word (e.g., Basketball = Umupila wakuminwe)
Translation by describing the item or concept (e.g., cardigan = icakufwala campepo)
Adopting the word from another language directly (e.g., Computer = Kompyuta)
Word-for-word translation keeping original meaning

questions: Alula imiseela ili pesamba, uitwale mucibemba. Ubomfye inshila shimo-shimo isho wasambilila: a. When I visited Chanda this morning she complained of severe headache, b. Chanda was appointed head girl at our school, however, she preferred just being an ordinary pupil, c. Making clay vessels was Bwalya's favorite project in school, d. Clay vessels must be handled with care because they can easily break
Alula ii miseela ili peesamba uitwaile mucisungu: i. Pamaliketi ileelo pali ifisabo ifyalekana-lekana, ii. Bana maliketi baleshitisha amapeela, imyembe, ifitanga-menshi na ifibimbi

sub_topics.title: Ukumfwa na Ukulanda - Ifishinka na Ifyakwelenganya (Listening and Speaking - Facts and Opinions)
competences: Ukupusanya ifishinka na ifyakwelenga mulyashi lyakulanshanya

teacher_steps: Eba abasambi babili ukuipeelesha ukusendamo ulubali mukulanshanya
Eba abasambi babelenge ilyashi lyakulanshanya ilili peesamba
Eba abasambi bapusanye ifishinka na ifyakwelenganya
learner_steps: Abasambi babelenge ilyashi umuli ifishinka na ifyakwelenganya
instructional_blocks.short_notes: Learners read a dialogue between Mulenga and Katongo discussing whether women can be traditional leaders (bashilika). They identify facts and opinions expressed in the conversation.

teacher_steps: Eba abasambi balondolole ifishinka na ifyakwelenganya
instructional_blocks.short_notes: Learners present and classify facts versus opinions from the dialogue. Example: Fact - Namaayo kuti aaba umushilika; Opinion - tekuti abe umushilika.

sub_topics.short_notes: Facts (ifishinka) are statements that can be verified as true or false. Opinions (ifyakwelenganya) are personal views or beliefs. In the dialogue about women becoming traditional leaders, learners practice distinguishing between factual statements and personal opinions, and practice respectful communication including proper turn-taking in conversation.

vocabulary.definition: opportunities, chances
seeds, offspring (used figuratively for children/results)
tears
from experience, from the river/source of knowledge
from an anthill (figurative for accumulated knowledge)
to learn from, to borrow knowledge

questions: Ilyashi lyabelengwa bwino ilyapafishinka na ifyakwelenganya (Story read correctly with facts and opinions identified)

sub_topics.title: Ukubelenga na Ukumfwikisha - Ubusuma Bwamasambililo (Reading Comprehension - The Value of Education)
competences: Ukubelenga imilandu yamu fomu 1

teacher_steps: Eba cila musambi ukubelenga umulandu
learner_steps: Cila musambi abelenge ilyashi lyamu fomu 1 bwino
instructional_blocks.short_notes: Learners read the passage about the value of education - discussing how both traditional schools (insaka for men, icibwanse for women) and modern schools offer various forms of knowledge.

teacher_steps: Eba cila musambi asuke ameepusho ayalekana-lekana bwino
learner_steps: Cila musambi asuke ameepusho ayalekana-lekana bwino
instructional_blocks.short_notes: Learners answer comprehension questions about the passage.

sub_topics.short_notes: The passage 'Ubusuma Bwamasambililo' (The Value of Education) discusses both traditional and modern education systems in Zambia. Traditional education happened in insaka (for boys) and icibwanse (for girls) where skills like farming, craftsmanship, cooking, and cultural values were taught. Modern education continues this tradition and adds formal subjects. Education leads to national development, employment, and improved quality of life.

vocabulary.definition: Traditional meeting place/school for men and boys
Traditional gathering place/school for women and girls
Attendance, presence
Progress, development, advancement

questions: Mulandunshi uukankala uo icipandwa cakwambavilelandapo?
Amashiwi yakutila ukupenda intanda kwandwa yalola mukuti: A. Kuti waikatwa, B. Teti tukumemo ukulondolola fyonse, C. Tukaiteka no kusambilila, D. Tukasansamuka
Londolola ubupusano bwaba pansaka elyo na ifibwanse.
Londolola imilandu ibili ilumbwilwe mu mulandu ico ukusambilila kwawamina.
Cinshi cingalenga ukuti icaalo naco citumpuluke ngacakuti abantu baciko nabasambilila?

sub_topics.title: Ukusupawila Umulandu (Writing a Summary)
competences: Ukusupawila imilandu iipi

teacher_steps: Biika abasambi mumabuumba
Balanshanye pafikomo fyakwipifishamo umulandu mucipande
Umwiminishi alondolole ifikomo filanshanishiwe
instructional_blocks.short_notes: Learners discuss the main points/key ideas from a passage and how to express them in a summary.

instructional_blocks.hook: Ukufumya na ukuleemba ifikomo ifikankaala ukufuma mumulandu upeelwe
teacher_steps: Eba cila musambi afumye ifikomo ifikankaala mumulandu upeelwe
learner_steps: Cila musambi afumye ifikomo ifikankaala mumulandu upeelwe
instructional_blocks.short_notes: Learners identify and extract key ideas from a given passage.

instructional_blocks.hook: Ukuleemba bwino umulandu upeelwe mucipande cimo
teacher_steps: Eba cila musambi aleembe umulandu bwino mu cipande cimo
learner_steps: Cila musambi aleembe umulandu mucipande cimo
instructional_blocks.short_notes: Learners write the summary of the given passage in one paragraph.

sub_topics.short_notes: Summary writing guidelines: Read and understand the passage; Write in short but complete sentences forming one paragraph; Identify key ideas clearly; Write only what is essential; Use clear and understandable language; Do not repeat the same ideas; Do not write only opinions without facts; Use synonyms where appropriate to avoid direct copying.

questions: Umulandu upeelwe wasupawilwa bwino mucipande cimo (The given passage was summarized correctly in one paragraph)

sub_topics.title: Ukusobolola Icibemba - Ilandile na Landilwa (Grammar - Direct and Indirect Speech)
competences: Ukubomfya ilandile (Use direct speech correctly)

instructional_blocks.hook: Ukulanshanya pa bupilibulo na imibomfeshe ya ilandile
teacher_steps: Biika abasambi mumabumba
Eba abasambi balanshanye ubupilibulo na imibomfeshe ya ilandile
Eba umwiminishi wa ibumba ukulondolola
instructional_blocks.short_notes: Learners discuss the uses and features of direct speech (ilandile) using examples.

instructional_blocks.hook: Ukufwaya imiseela ya ilandile mumilandu ipeelwe
teacher_steps: Biika abasambi mumabumba
Eba abasambi babelenge na ukufwaya imiseela ya ilandile mumilandu ipeelwe
instructional_blocks.short_notes: Learners find sentences in direct speech from given passages.

teacher_steps: Eba abasambi babiike amatoni mumiseela ya ilandile iili peesamba
learner_steps: Babiike amatoni mu miseela ya ilandile
instructional_blocks.short_notes: Learners add correct punctuation marks to direct speech sentences.

teacher_steps: Eba cila musambi apanga na ukuleemba imiseela ya ilandile
learner_steps: Cila musambi apange na ukuleemba imiseela ya ilandile
instructional_blocks.short_notes: Learners compose their own sentences in direct speech.

sub_topics.short_notes: Ilandile (Direct Speech): Used to report exactly what someone said. It has two parts: Kalanda (the speaker reference) and ifilandilwe (the words spoken). Direct speech uses first-person pronouns like 'ine, ifwe'. It uses punctuation: inverted commas to open and close the quote, a comma before the quote, a capital letter after opening inverted commas, and a full stop before closing inverted commas. It is used in present tense.

vocabulary.definition: Direct speech - reporting exactly what someone said, using quotation marks
Indirect speech - reporting what someone said without quoting them directly
Punctuation marks used in writing
The speaker/narrator reference in direct speech construction

questions: Leemba bwino imiseela ipeelwe muli ilandile: 1. banakulu basosa ati baayakwifwe mukutapa ameenshi, 2. kwena nakutalala saana efyailishanya abaana, 3. loleleeni twalaya mukulima nga kabalika efyasosa baMulilo, 4. Katongo atiila ati ndeesamba, 5. Mulindwa aatile Sampa alitemwa ukulya cungamu

sub_topics.title: Amashiwi Yakwata Ubupilibulo Bwafisama - Amapinda (Figurative Language - Proverb Completions)
competences: Ukubomfya amapinda (Use proverbs with two parts appropriately)

instructional_blocks.hook: Ukulanshanya ubupilibulo na imibomfeshe ya amapinda
teacher_steps: Biika basambi mumabumba
Eba abasambi balanshanye ubupilibulo na imibomfeshe ya amapinda
instructional_blocks.short_notes: Learners discuss the meaning and uses of amapinda (proverbs/idioms with two parts).

instructional_blocks.hook: Ukubiika amapinda mumabumba ukukonka na ifyo yabomba
teacher_steps: Biika abasambi mumabumba
Eba abasambi babiike amapinda mumabumba ukukonka na ifyo yabomba
instructional_blocks.short_notes: Learners classify proverbs according to their functions/uses.

instructional_blocks.hook: Ukufwaya na ukuleemba amapinda ayalungatene na ifilecitika
teacher_steps: Eba cila musambi afwaye amapinda ayalungatene na ifilecitika
learner_steps: Cila musambi afwaye amapinda ayalungatene na ifilecitika
instructional_blocks.short_notes: Learners find appropriate proverbs that match given situations.

instructional_blocks.hook: Ukufwaya na ukuleemba ifilecitika umwingabomfiwa bwino amapinda
teacher_steps: Eba abasambi bafwaye na ukuleemba ifilecitika umwingabomfiwa amapinda
learner_steps: Bafwaye na ukuleemba ifilecitika umwingabomfiwa amapinda yafwailwe
instructional_blocks.short_notes: Learners identify situations where given proverbs would be appropriately used.

sub_topics.short_notes: Amapinda (Proverbs/Idioms with Two Parts) have two structural parts: ukutampa (beginning/setup) and ukupwishishisha (completion/response). They are used to: convey cultural wisdom, teach moral lessons, resolve disputes, preserve African traditions, entertain through songs and stories, and demonstrate inherited intelligence. Examples: Ukupoka icinsenda ku nkoko (taking a chick from a hen) - about difficulty; cinci wababili, tecinci uli weka (it concerns two, not one alone).

vocabulary.definition: Two-part proverbs/idioms used to convey wisdom and cultural values in Icibemba
The first/opening part of a two-part proverb
The completion/second part of a two-part proverb

questions: Pwishishisha amapinda yapeelwe: 1. Uwenda mumushitu, ……, 2. Uwakakaana ukwela, ……, 3. Akacila kambushi, ……, 4. Imiti ipalamene, ……, 5. Umuto walupwa tawitika, ……

sub_topics.title: Ukumfwa na Ukulanda - Ukulanda Kucintubwingi (Public Speaking)
competences: Ukulanda ilyashi kwi bumba pamutwe upeelwe

instructional_blocks.hook: Ukufwaya ifyebo fyakulanda mulyashi pamutwe upeelwe
teacher_steps: Eba abasambi balanshanye mumabumba ubukankaala bwa kulanda kucintubwingi
Eba abasambi basale kalemba webumba
Eba bakalemba ukufuma mumabumba bemenine kuntanshi na ukulondolola ifyo balanshenye
learner_steps: Bakalemba mumabumba beminine kuntanshi na ukulondolola ifyo balanshenye
instructional_blocks.short_notes: Learners brainstorm key points for a speech on an assigned topic, discussing the importance and value of public speaking.

teacher_steps: Lemba pacipampa amafunde yakukonka pakulanda kucintu bwingi
Eba abasambi balembe pafyo ayaamafunde yenga bafwa ukulanda bwino kucintubwingi
Eba abasambi basale uwakulanda ilyashi pali bamineti basaano
learner_steps: Balembe ifyo amafunde yenga baafwa ukulanda bwino mumabumba
Abasambi basale uwakulanda ilyashi pebumba, becila pali bamineti basaano
instructional_blocks.short_notes: Learners prepare and deliver a 5-minute group speech on an assigned topic following public speaking guidelines.

sub_topics.short_notes: Public speaking (ukulanda kucintubwingi) guidelines: Know your audience; Know your topic well; Make your audience follow along (ask questions, use examples); Speak clearly and confidently; Rehearse your points; Discuss how to find content for the speech; Deliver the speech within a time limit (5 minutes). Key skills: preparation, organization of ideas, confidence, clarity, and audience engagement.

vocabulary.definition: A large group/audience; public speaking context
Giving a speech/presentation
Being embarrassed, shy, or feeling ashamed
Bride price (cultural practice discussed in reading passage)
A certain type of cultural practice/bride price token

questions: Ifyebo fyakubomfya mulyashi pamutwe upeelwe fya fwaiwa bwino bwino (Key points for the speech on assigned topic found correctly)
Ukulanda ilyashi kwibumba pamutwe uupelwe bwino (Group speech delivered correctly on the assigned topic)

sub_topics.title: Ukubelenga na Ukumfwikisha - Ukucindika Imitundu Imbi (Reading - Respecting Other Ethnic Groups)
competences: Ukubelenga imilandu iyapusana-pusana iilingile ba Form 1

teacher_steps: Peela abasambi icitabo umuli ilyashi lyakubelenga
Sonta abasambi bamo-bamo abalabelenga ilyashi mukwikatisha
Eba abasambi babelenge umulandu cila umo na umo mumutalalila
learner_steps: Baibelengele umulandu cila umo na umo mumutalalila
instructional_blocks.short_notes: Learners read the speech 'Ukulanda pa Kuilama' (Speaking about Self-Discipline) and answer comprehension questions.

teacher_steps: Eba abasambi baasuke ameepusho yapeelwe
Eba abasambi balete ifitabo mukucongesha
Pituluka pamo na abasambi mumaasuko yameepusho yacipeelwa
instructional_blocks.short_notes: Learners answer comprehension questions about the speech on self-discipline.

sub_topics.short_notes: The reading passage 'Ukulanda pa Kuilama' discusses self-discipline. Self-discipline (ukuilama) involves: resisting what is wrong, making good decisions, not settling for comfort, and pursuing goals even through challenges. Key strategies: setting achievable goals, establishing a routine, avoiding distractions, seeking support from trusted people, and viewing challenges as opportunities. Additionally, the topic 'Ukucindika Imitundu Imbi' discusses how Zambia's diversity of languages and cultures is a strength, and how each ethnic group has its own foods, customs, and practices that should be respected.

vocabulary.definition: Self-discipline - the ability to control one's behavior and resist temptation
To act on impulse, to rush into things without thinking
Strategies, steps, methods to achieve a goal
Cultural practices, way of life
Ethnic groups, tribes, clans

questions: Cinshi ili lyashi lileelandapo? a) Ubucindami bwa isukulu, b) Ifyakucimfya amafya mubuumi, c) Umulimo ukuilama kwakwata mubuumi, d) Ubunonshi bwakubombela pamo
Ukulingana na ilyashi, cinshi cipusanya abo abakwanisha mumikalile yabo nabalya abatontonkanyafye ukuti bakakwanisha?
Bunonshinshi bumo ubulumbwilwe mulyashi ubwakuilama?
Ninshilanshi imo igalenga wafilwa ukuilama? a) Ukusuminafye fyonse, b) Ukutantika inshita yafyo uli na ukubomba, c) Ukukanakwata amafya ayali yonse, d) Ukwimika ubuyo
Cinshi cinga bomba nga ubulalo bwakukufumya apo uli pali ndakai na ukukwabushisha kwishilya kunshiku shileeisa?

sub_topics.title: Ukulemba Ilyashi Lyakulondolola Inama (Descriptive Writing - Describing an Animal)
competences: Ilyashi lyakulondolola inama (Write a composition describing an animal)

teacher_steps: Biika abasambi mumabumba balanshanye ifisangwa mumilembele yabulondoloshi
Eba abasambi basale kalemba uulepeela ubulondoloshi kuntanshi
instructional_blocks.short_notes: Learners discuss the features of descriptive writing.

teacher_steps: Lanshanyeni ifileekabilwa ukusanshiwamo mukulemba ubulondoloshi bwafinama
Eba abasambi balembe ilyashi lya kulondolola inama ukulingana na ilipusho lipeelwe
learner_steps: Balanshanye na bakafundisha ifileekabilwa pakulemba ubulondoloshi bwafinama
Balembe mufitabo umo-umo ilyashi lyakulondolola inama ukulingana na ilipusho bapeelwe
instructional_blocks.short_notes: Learners write a descriptive composition about an animal following descriptive writing conventions.

sub_topics.short_notes: Descriptive writing (ubulondoloshi) uses the senses (sight, smell, touch, sound) to paint a vivid picture. Techniques: Use vivid adjectives (batungi-weshina) and adverbs (batungi-wakacita); show rather than tell (e.g., don't just say 'alitiina' - describe trembling hands and pounding heart); create a picture in the reader's mind so they feel they are there. Use proverbs and figurative language to enrich descriptions.

vocabulary.definition: Description - writing that uses sensory details to paint a picture
Life, vitality - giving life to a description
Adverbs - words that describe how actions are performed
Adjectives - words that describe nouns
To touch/feel - tactile sense used in descriptive writing

questions: Kafundisha apeele abasambi ukulemba ubulondoshi bwa finama ifyo baishiba.

sub_topics.title: Ukusobolola Icibemba - Landilwa (Grammar - Indirect Speech)
competences: Ukupanga imiseela muli landilwa (Construct sentences in indirect speech)

teacher_steps: Lemba imiseela pa cipampa
Biika abasambi mumabumba na ukubeeba balembe imiseela ili pacipampa elyo basale iili muli landilwa
Eba bakalemba wamabumba beminine kuntanshi na ukupeela imiseela ili muli landilwa
learner_steps: Bakalemba wamabumba beminine kuntanshi na ukupeela imiseela ili muli Landilwa
instructional_blocks.short_notes: Learners identify and extract indirect speech sentences from given examples.

instructional_blocks.hook: Ukubika amatoni ayalingile mumiseela ili muli landilwa
teacher_steps: Eba abasambi balembe imiseela ine muli Landilwa na ukubomfya amatoni ayalinga
learner_steps: Balembe imiseela ine muli Landilwa ukubomfya amatoni ayalinga
instructional_blocks.short_notes: Learners use correct punctuation in indirect speech.

teacher_steps: Eba abasambi balembe imiseela isaano muli Landilwa
instructional_blocks.short_notes: Learners write five original sentences in indirect speech.

instructional_blocks.hook: Twaleeni imiseela ili muli Landilwa muli Ilandile kabili twaleeni imiseela ili muli Ilandile muli Landilwa
teacher_steps: Eba abasambi balembe imiseela itatu muli Landilwa elyo baitwale muli Ilandile
Eba abasambi balembe imiseela ibili muli Ilandile elyo baitwale muli Landilwa
learner_steps: Balembe imiseela itatu muli Landilwa elyo baitwale muli Ilandile
Balembe imiseela ibili muli Ilandile elyo baitwale muli Landilwa
instructional_blocks.short_notes: Learners convert between direct and indirect speech.

sub_topics.short_notes: Landilwa (Indirect Speech): Used to report what someone said without quoting directly; no inverted commas used; pronoun changes occur (na → a, wa → a); verb tense changes to show the speech occurred in the past. Example: Ilandile: Besa atile, 'Naalitemwa ukulya imyembe.' → Landilwa: Besa atiile aalitemwa ukulya imyembe.

vocabulary.definition: Indirect speech - reporting what someone said without using their exact words or quotation marks
Direct speech - quoting someone's exact words using inverted commas

questions: Kafundisha apeele abasambi imiseela muli landilwa batwale muli ilandile.

sub_topics.title: Ukumfwa na Ukulanda - Ukupaashanya (Listening and Speaking - Debating)
competences: Bapaashanye pamutwe uuli onse uwingapeelwa (Debate on any given topic)

instructional_blocks.hook: Ukupaashanya pa milandu iibalingile iyakuma ubwikashi bwa bantu
teacher_steps: Ipusha abasambi ubupilibulo bweshiwi 'ukupaashanya'
Ipusha abasambi balondolole intampulo shikonkwa pakupaashanya
Lundapo ubulondoloshi pamulandu wakupaashanya
Biika abasambi mumabumba babe basaano-basaano
Peela abasambi imitwe yakupaashanyapo
Peela abasambi akashita kakuya mukufwailisha ifishinka
Eba amabumba yalekaana na ukusumina kumitwe yalekana-lekana bapeelwe akashita kakupaashanya kuntanshi
learner_steps: Mumabumba ayalekaana na ukusumina batapaashanye pamitwe bapeelwe
instructional_blocks.short_notes: Learners prepare and conduct a formal debate on topics related to social issues. One team argues for the motion, the other argues against. Teams research their arguments and take turns presenting.

sub_topics.short_notes: Ukupaashanya (Debate): A formal discussion where two sides argue for or against a motion. Key features: There is a topic/motion (umutwe); one side argues for (ukusuminisha), the other argues against (ukukaanya); each side must present evidence and logical arguments. Debate procedures include: opening statements, rebuttals, and closing arguments.

vocabulary.definition: Debating - a formal structured argument on a given topic with two opposing sides
Proposing/arguing for a motion
Opposition - arguing against a motion
The debate topic/motion

questions: imilandu iibalingile iyakuma ku bwikashi bwabantu yapaashanishipo bwino (Social issues debated correctly)

sub_topics.title: Ukubelenga na Ukumfwikisha - HIV na AIDS (Reading Comprehension - HIV and AIDS)
competences: Ukubelenga imilandu iilingile ba Fomu 1 iyapusana-pusana

instructional_blocks.hook: Ukubelenga ilyashi lyaumulandu wa Mwansa na HIV na AIDS
teacher_steps: Peela abasambi ifitabo umuli ilyashi lyakubelenga
Sonta abasambi bamo-bamo abalabelenga ilyashi mukwikatisha
Eba abasambi babelenge umulandu cila umo na umo mumutima
Afwilisha abasambi mumibelengele isuma
learner_steps: Baibelengele umulandu cila umo na umo mu mutima
instructional_blocks.short_notes: Learners read about Mwansa's journey of learning about HIV/AIDS and how she became an advocate for health awareness in her community.

teacher_steps: Eba abasambi baasuke ameepusho yakusalapo mukulondolafye pakanwa
Eba abasambi balondolole eco bapeelela ubo bwasuko
Eba abasambi balembe ameepusho ayashele mufitabo fyabo
instructional_blocks.short_notes: Learners answer varied comprehension questions about the HIV/AIDS passage.

sub_topics.short_notes: The passage about HIV/AIDS covers: HIV (akashishi ka HIV) weakens the immune system; HIV can be transmitted through unprotected intercourse, sharing needles, and mother-to-child transmission during breastfeeding; People with HIV can live long healthy lives if they know their status early and take medication; Stigma prevents people from getting tested; The importance of advocating for HIV awareness and testing in communities.

vocabulary.definition: Being afraid, fear
Human Immunodeficiency Virus/Acquired Immunodeficiency Syndrome - a disease that weakens the immune system
To mock, stigmatize, or discriminate against someone
To encourage, motivate, advocate

questions: Bushe bukankala shi ubulimi bwakwata mu caalo?
Mulandu nshi amataba yaabeela icilimwa icacindama mu Zambia?
Elenganya mu caalo mwapona icilala, bushe ubuteeko kuti bwayafwilisha shani abalimi ukubomfya ubutantiko ya FISP?
Lemba amaafya yabili ayalumbwilwe mu mulandu ayo abalimi banoono bashingwana nayo.
Bushe ubulimi bwa fisabo fimbi ukucila pamataba, bukankalanshi bwakwata mukulundulula ubukumu bwa caalo?

sub_topics.title: Ukulemba Ilyashi Lyakupaashanya (Writing an Argumentative Essay)
competences: Ukulemba ilyashi lyakupaashanya

instructional_blocks.hook: Ukulanshanya pamilembele na ifikomo fisangwa mulyashi lyakupaashanya
teacher_steps: Lemba ilipusho pa cipampa
Eba abasambi ukukopolola ilipusho mufitabo fyabo
Biika abasambi mumabumba
Eba abasambi mumabumba yabo basale bakalemba
Eba abasambi balanshanye palipusho lipeelwe
Lundapo pabulondoloshi bupeelwe
instructional_blocks.short_notes: Learners discuss and identify features and structure of an argumentative essay.

teacher_steps: Lemba ilipusho pacipampa
Eba basambi bakopolole ilipusho lili pacipampa mufitabo fyabo
Londolola ifilekabilwa abasambi ukukonka mulipusho
Conga ifitabo fyabasambi
learner_steps: Baasuke nga cakuti nabomfwikisha ifilekabilwa ukukonkwa mulipusho
instructional_blocks.short_notes: Learners write a complete argumentative essay on an assigned topic.

sub_topics.short_notes: Argumentative essay (ilyashi lyakupaashanya) structure: 1. Ukutendeka (Introduction) - state the topic briefly, show your side, give a preview of key points. 2. Ilyashi mukushika (Body - 3 or 4 paragraphs) - each point has its own paragraph; provide supporting evidence; explain how evidence supports your argument. 3. Ukusondwelela (Conclusion) - briefly restate your position; summarize key arguments; give your final recommendation or call to action. Use persuasive language and respectful tone. Use connectives: eico, kanshi, lubali lumbi, nangu cibe ifyo, mukulundapo.

vocabulary.definition: Debate/argumentation
To convince/persuade the audience
Conclusion of an essay or speech

questions: Peela abasambi ukulemba ilyashi ili pakupaashanya (Write an argumentative essay)

sub_topics.title: Ukusobolola Icibemba - Amashiwi Yalanga Ukucitika na Ukucitwa (Grammar - Active and Passive Voice)
competences: ukubomfya amashiwi yalanga ukucitika na ukucitwa kwa fintu (Use active and passive voice)

instructional_blocks.hook: Ukulondolola ubupilibulo na umulimo wamashiwi yalanga ukucitika kwacintu na ukucitwa kwa cintu
teacher_steps: Eba abasambi umo umo balelondolola pakanwa ubupilibulo na umulimo ubomba amashiwi yalanga ukucitika na ukucitwa kwa cintu
Lundapo pabulondoloshi abasambi balepeela pakuti bomfwikishe
Eba abasambi beepushe ameepusho apo bashumfwikishe
learner_steps: Balondolole umo-umo pakanwa ubupilibulo na umulimo wamashiwi yalanga ukucitika na ukucitwa kwafintu
Baleelemba mufitabo fyabo ubulondoloshi baleumfwa kuli kafundisha
instructional_blocks.short_notes: Teacher explains active and passive voice; learners discuss and note the differences.

instructional_blocks.hook: Ukupusanya amashiwi yalanga ukucitika kwa fintu elyo na amashiwi yalanga ukucitwa kwafintu
teacher_steps: Biika abasambi mumabumba
Eba abasambi ukuti, cilaibumba balanshanye pabupusano bwaba pamashiwi yalanga ukucitika kwa fintu elyo na ukucitwa kwafintu
learner_steps: Balanshanye pabupusano bwaba pamashiwi yalanga ukucitika kwa fintu elyo na ukucitwa kwafintu
instructional_blocks.short_notes: Learners compare and contrast active and passive voice.

instructional_blocks.hook: Ukusanga amashiwi yalanga ifyo icintu cilecitika elyo na amashiwi yalanga ifyo icintu cicitilwe
teacher_steps: Peela abasambi ilyashi umuli amashiwi yalanga ifyo ifintu filecitika elyo na ayalanga ifyo ifintu filecitwa
Eba cila musambi ukufwaya amashiwi ukufuma mulyashi
learner_steps: Bapoke ifitabo ifikwete ilyashi lilekabilwa ukubelenga
Babelenge ilyashi umuli amashiwi yalekabilwa ukusanga
Batantike bwino mu fitabo fyabo amashiwi ayalekabilwa
instructional_blocks.short_notes: Learners identify and classify active and passive voice words in a given passage.

instructional_blocks.hook: Ukupanga imiseela ukubomfya amashiwi yalanga ukucitika kwacintu elyo na ayalanga ifyo icintu cicitilwe
teacher_steps: Eba abasambi balembe mufitabo fyabo imiseela isaano iyalaba mumashiwi yalanga ukucitika kwacintu elyo na ayalanga ifyo icintu cilecitwa
learner_steps: Balembe imiseela isaano isaano mufitabo fyamo ukubomfya amashiwi ayalanga ukucitika kwafintu elyo na ayalanga ifyo icintu cicitilwe
instructional_blocks.short_notes: Learners write sentences in both active and passive voice.

instructional_blocks.hook: ukucinja imiseela ukufuma mumashiwi yalanga ukucitika kwafintu ukutwala mumashiwi yalanga ifyo icintu cicitilwe
teacher_steps: Lemba umuseela wafyakucita pacipampa
Londolwela bwino abasambi pafilekabilwa ukubombelwapo
Eba abasambi nomba bacinje mufitabo fyabo imiseela iilekabilwa ukucinja
learner_steps: Bacinje imiseela ipeelwe ukulingana na ilipusho
instructional_blocks.short_notes: Learners convert sentences from active to passive voice and vice versa.

sub_topics.short_notes: Active Voice (Amashiwi yalanga ukucitika kwafintu): The subject performs the action. Example: Mutale aletema icimuti (Mutale is cutting a tree). Passive Voice (Amashiwi yalanga ukucitwa kwafintu): The action is performed on the subject. Example: Icimuti ciletemwa na Mutale (The tree is being cut by Mutale). In Icibemba, passive verbs are formed by adding 'wa/ilwa/ulwa' to the verb root. Active voice examples: lemba, leeta, fuluka; Passive voice: lembwa, letwa, fulukwa.

vocabulary.definition: Active voice - verbs showing the subject performing the action (e.g., lemba, leeta, seka)
Passive voice - verbs showing action done to the subject (e.g., lembwa, letwa, sekwa)

questions: Eba abasambi bacinje imiseela ili peesamba ukufuma mumashiwi yalanga ukucitika kwacintu, ukutwala mumashiwi yalanga ifyo icintu cacitwa: 1. Ba Mulilo balekokota amafupa yankoko, 2. Mukonde aaleta isaka lyabunga, 3. Kangwa aapanta umupila, 4. Chisala aalefunda abasambi, 5. Mweshi aalecapa ifyakufwala

sub_topics.title: Ukumfwa na Ukulanda - Inyimbo Shapali Ndakai (Contemporary Music)
competences: Balondolole ubukankala bwa nyimbo shacikaaya pamofye na ishapali ndakai (Explain the importance of traditional and contemporary songs)

instructional_blocks.hook: Ukulanshanya na ukulondolola ubukankala bwa nyimbo shapali ndakai
teacher_steps: Biika abasambi mumabumba
Eba abasambi balanshanye ubukankala bwa nyimbo shapali ndaki
Eba abasambi basale kaleemba webumba
Eba bakaleemba ukufuma mumabumba bemenine kuntanshi na ukulondolola ifyo balanshenye
learner_steps: Bakalemba mumabumba beminine kuntanshi na ukulondolola ifyo balanshenye
instructional_blocks.short_notes: Learners discuss and present the importance of contemporary songs.

instructional_blocks.hook: Ukupelulula amafundisho yali mu nyimbo shapali ndaki
teacher_steps: Tambisha abasambi ubunkolanya panyimbo shapali ndakai nangu ukubofwiisha icilimba
Eba abasambi balembe amafunde yali munyimbo bomfwile na ubunkolanya batambile
Conga ifitabo fyabasambi
Eba abasambi balande amaasuko baacilemba mufitabo
learner_steps: Balondolole na ukulanshanya pakanwa pamo na kafundisha
instructional_blocks.short_notes: Learners listen to or sing contemporary songs, identify and discuss the moral lessons contained in them.

teacher_steps: Biika abasambi mumabumba
Suminisha abasambi bafume panse na ukupituluka munyimbo shapali ndakai
Eba abasambi bembe inyimbo shapali ndakai mumabumba
Eba abasambi balondolole ifunde lili mulwimbo baimba
instructional_blocks.short_notes: Learners perform contemporary songs in groups and identify the moral lessons.

sub_topics.short_notes: Contemporary Zambian music (inyimbo shapali ndakai) is modern music enjoyed today. It mixes traditional Zambian sounds with new styles like hip hop, R&B, and Afrobeat. Popular artists include Yo Maps, Macky 2, and Slapdee. Many sing in local languages like Icibemba and Nyanja. Some artists also sing gospel music. With phones and the internet, Zambian music is now heard worldwide. Music serves important social functions: entertainment, cultural preservation, education, and economic opportunity.

vocabulary.definition: Contemporary/modern songs of today
Traditional/old songs from the past
Income, earnings, economic benefit
Cultural practices, traditions of various ethnic groups
Today, the present time, contemporary

questions: Lumbula ubukankala bwaba mukwimba inyimbo shapali ndakai.
Cinshi caalengele abakashana ukwaluka amatontonkanyo yabo palwakwimba?
Fisambililonshi abakashana balumbwilwe mumulandu baalesambilila?
Londolola amashiwi yali peesamba ukulingana na ifyo yabomfeshiwe mumulandu: (i) Ifyamabwenge, (ii) Ukusowesha, (iii) Ukusongwa

sub_topics.title: Ukulemba Ilyashi Lyakulondolola Umuntu/Icintu (Descriptive Writing - Describing a Person/Thing)
competences: Ukulemba ilyashi lyakulondolola umuntu/icintu (Descriptive Writing)

instructional_blocks.hook: Ukulanshanya pafikomo ifikankala ifyakukonka pakulemba ilyashi lyakulondolola umuntu
teacher_steps: Biika abasambi mumabumba balanshanye ifyo ilyashi lyakulondolola umuntu lilembwa
Eba abasambi basale kaleemba uulepeela ubulondoloshi kuntanshi
Eba bakaleemba wamabumba beese kuntashi na ukulondolola ifyo bacilanshanya
Lundapo pabulondoloshai abasambi bapeele
learner_steps: Baakalemba baye kuntanshi na ukulondolola ifyo bacilanshanya
instructional_blocks.short_notes: Learners discuss and identify key features of descriptive writing about a person.

teacher_steps: Eba abasambi baleembe ilyashi umo balalondolola umuntu
Eba abasambi baleete ifitabo mukucongesha
learner_steps: Baleembe ilyashi ukulingana na ilipusho lipeelwe
instructional_blocks.short_notes: Learners write their own descriptive composition about a person.

sub_topics.short_notes: A descriptive composition of a person/thing (ilyashi lyakulondolola umuntu nangu icintu) presents vivid description to bring the subject to life for the reader. Key features: Use vivid adjectives and adverbs; Focus only on what is important, not everything; Use comparisons to help describe; Read model descriptions to learn from other writers; Pay attention to appearance, character, behavior, and activities. Use appropriate punctuation. The composition must have: introduction, body, and conclusion.

vocabulary.definition: Vivid/colorful descriptive language that creates a clear picture
Descriptive composition about a person or thing

questions: Ukulemba ilyashi lyakulondolola umuntu bwino (Write a descriptive composition about a person correctly)

sub_topics.title: Ukusobolola Icibemba - Ifiunda (Grammar - Interjections)
competences: Ukubombfya imisango yafiunda iyapusana-pusana mumiseela (Use different types of interjections in sentences)

teacher_steps: Biika abasambi mumabumba
Lemba ilipusho pacipampa ililekabila abasambi ukulondolola ubupilibulo bwafiunda
Eba abasambi basale kalemba pali cila ibumba
Eba abasambi balanshanye ubupilibulo bwafiunda
instructional_blocks.short_notes: Learners discuss the meaning and uses of interjections (ifiunda).

instructional_blocks.hook: Ukufwaya ifiunda ukulingana na ilangi elyo na imibeele
teacher_steps: Lemba ilipusho pacipampa ililekabila abasambi ukupeela amashiwi yafiunda ayalanda pamalangi elyo na ayalanda pamibeele
learner_steps: Bakopolole ameepusho yali pacipampa mufitabo fyabo
instructional_blocks.short_notes: Learners find interjections related to colors and properties/qualities.

instructional_blocks.hook: ukupanga imiseela ukubomfya imisango ya fiunda iyapusana-pusana
teacher_steps: Lemba ifiunda fyamashiwi pacipampa ifyalekana-lekana
Eba abasambi balembe imiseela isaano-isano ukubomfya ifiunda ifili pacipampa
instructional_blocks.short_notes: Learners compose sentences using given interjections.

sub_topics.short_notes: Ifiunda (Interjections): Word parts (morphemes) added to verbs or adjectives that intensify their meaning. In Icibemba, interjections are suffixes from verb or adjective roots. They express sensory qualities (color, texture, sound, etc.). Example: icela cakashika cee! (A very white shell!). Interjections are written with an exclamation mark [!]. Categories: colors (Ukufiita - blackness, Ukukashika - whiteness, Ukubuuta - brownness) and properties (Ukukosa - hardness, Ukuuma - heaviness, Ukulula - lightness, Ukununka - smell, Ukwiminina - straightness).

vocabulary.definition: Interjections - word parts expressing sensory qualities, written with exclamation mark
Blackness/darkness (color interjection)
Whiteness/brightness (color interjection)
Brownish color (color interjection)
Hardness (property interjection)

questions: Panga ifiunda ukufuma kuli ayamashiwi yabomfeshiwe mumiseela: a. Bacimuma ulupi lwalila na, b. Inkoko naibola inunkile na, c. Acifubula isaka lyamataba na, d. Inkuba ibyatile bushiku bonse yacilati na, e. Baciposa ilibwe mumenshi lyalila na

Lemba ubupilibulo bwa ishi insoselo shili peesamba: a. Umulandu muume, b. Ukuninika iciwa pacishiki, c. Ukuleta ulwisula mushi, d. Ukupaapa kalama, e. Ukushala impondo, f. Ukwendela ulwisha, g. Ukusonkomona ishimu kulupako, h. Ukuilapwila umulilo pamakasa
